description: Reduce limit from top-10 products result
last_result_summary: Question: What are the top 10 products by total sales revenue? Objective: Join order_items with products, sum total_amount per product, order desc limit 10. Result: 10 rows with columns: product_name, total_revenue. Answered: Nike Running Shoes led with Rs 320K, Adidas Ultraboost second at Rs 280K, Sony Alpha Camera third at Rs 210K, Mamaearth Onion Shampoo fourth at Rs 185K, Philips Electric Kettle fifth at Rs 162K.
expected_rewritten_query: What are the top 5 products by total sales revenue?

description: Add status filter to Q4 orders result
current_query: Filter those to only cancelled orders
last_result_summary: Question: List all orders placed in Q4 2024. Objective: Filter orders where created_at between Oct 1 and Dec 31 2024. Result: 450 rows with columns: order_id, user_id, total_amount, status. Answered: Q4 2024 saw 450 orders totalling Rs 2.3M across all statuses — delivered, shipped, pending, and cancelled.
expected_rewritten_query: How many cancelled orders were placed in Q4 2024?

description: Switch category filter from Electronics to Sports
last_result_summary: Question: What products are in the Electronics category? Objective: Join products with categories where category_name = Electronics. Result: 24 rows with columns: product_name, brand_name, price. Answered: Found 24 electronics products including Sony Xperia 5 V, Philips Electric Kettle, and Samsung Smart TV.
expected_rewritten_query: What products are in the Sports category?

description: Shift time window back one week
last_result_summary: Question: How many orders were placed last week? Objective: Filter orders where created_at in the past 7 days, count order_ids. Result: 1 row. Answered: 87 orders were placed last week with a total revenue of Rs 430K and an average order value of Rs 4,942.
expected_rewritten_query: How many orders were placed the week before last week, and what was the total revenue?

description: Change GROUP BY from city to product category
current_query: Now group those by category instead
last_result_summary: Question: What is the total revenue by city? Objective: Join orders with users, group by city, sum total_amount. Result: 15 rows with columns: city, total_revenue. Answered: Mumbai led with Rs 1.8M total revenue, Delhi second at Rs 1.3M, Bangalore third at Rs 1.1M.
expected_rewritten_query: What is the total revenue broken down by product category?

description: Switch aggregation from order count to total revenue per product
current_query: Show total revenue instead of count
last_result_summary: Question: How many orders does each product appear in? Objective: Join order_items with products, count distinct order_ids per product, order desc. Result: 50 rows with columns: product_name, order_count. Answered: Adidas Ultraboost 22 appeared in 65 orders, most of any product. Nike Running Shorts appeared in 50 orders.
expected_rewritten_query: What is the total revenue generated by each product, ordered from highest to lowest?

description: Break annual revenue aggregate into monthly breakdown
current_query: Break it down month by month
last_result_summary: Question: What was the total revenue for 2024? Objective: Filter orders where year = 2024, sum total_amount. Result: 1 row. Answered: Total revenue for 2024 was Rs 18.4M across all orders placed during the year.
expected_rewritten_query: What was the total revenue for each month in 2024?

description: Drilldown into Adidas Ultraboost from top-products list
current_query: Tell me more about Adidas Ultraboost
last_result_summary: Question: What are the top 10 products by total units sold? Objective: Join order_items with product_variants and products, group by product name, sum quantity. Result: 10 rows with columns: product_name, total_units_sold. Answered: Adidas Ultraboost 22 led with 65 units, Nike Running Shorts second with 50 units, Sony Alpha A6400 Camera third with 47 units.
expected_rewritten_query: What are the total units sold, revenue, and order count for the Adidas Ultraboost 22 product?

description: Drilldown into Mumbai from city-level order count result
current_query: Show all orders from Mumbai specifically
last_result_summary: Question: How many orders came from each city? Objective: Join orders with users, group by city, count order_ids. Result: 12 rows with columns: city, order_count. Answered: Mumbai led with 234 orders, Delhi second with 189 orders, Bangalore third with 145 orders.
expected_rewritten_query: What are all the orders placed by users in Mumbai?

description: Drilldown: which top revenue brand had the most returns
current_query: Which of those brands had the most returns?
last_result_summary: Question: Which brands have the highest total sales revenue? Objective: Join order_items with products and brands, group by brand name, sum total_amount, order desc limit 5. Result: 5 rows with columns: brand_name, total_revenue. Answered: Nike led with Rs 890K, Adidas second at Rs 750K, Sony third at Rs 620K, Philips fourth at Rs 410K, Mamaearth fifth at Rs 380K.
expected_rewritten_query: Which of the top revenue brands — Nike, Adidas, Sony, Philips, and Mamaearth — had the highest number of returned orders?

description: Passthrough: new query with no prior context
current_query: How many users registered last month?
expected_rewritten_query: How many users registered last month?

description: Passthrough: unrelated query despite prior context existing
current_query: List all products with zero stock
last_result_summary: Question: What is the total revenue by product category? Objective: Join orders, order_items, products, categories; group by category name; sum total_amount. Result: 8 rows. Answered: Electronics dominated with Rs 4.2M revenue, Clothing second at Rs 2.1M, Home Decor third at Rs 1.5M.
expected_rewritten_query: List all products with zero stock

description: Passthrough: new business question despite prior context
current_query: What is the average order value?
last_result_summary: Question: Which are the top 5 cities by total revenue? Objective: Join orders with users, group by city, sum total_amount, order desc limit 5. Result: 5 rows. Answered: Mumbai topped with Rs 1.8M, Delhi second at Rs 1.3M, Bangalore third at Rs 1.1M.
expected_rewritten_query: What is the average order value?
